task.title: Local syntax check of COBOL and PL/I source that contains EXEC CICS statements
task.shortdesc: To perform a quick verification of your code, you can execute a local syntax check on your program as an initial form of validation. Performing a syntax check is faster than performing a full build, so you must use it when you want quick feedback on the correctness of your program. Any EXEC CICS statements that are embedded in COBOL and PL/I source can be automatically translated by your CICS translator before you run a syntax check.

taskbody.prereq: syntax checkingCOBOLPL/I source containing EXEC CICS statementsPL/I source containing EXEC CICS statementssyntax checkingCOBOLsyntax checkingBefore you run a local syntax check, you must enable the CICS translator. For more information on enabling CICS translation support, see .

steps.stepsection: To execute a local syntax check, follow these steps:

step.cmd: In the view, right-click on the resource you want to check the syntax of and click Syntax CheckLocal or press Ctrl+Alt+L.

taskbody.result: Errors found by the syntax checker are listed in the Remote Error List view.
taskbody.postreq: Double-click an error that is listed in the Remote Error List view to display the corresponding line of code in the LPEX Editor.
